From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

British trade unionist (1930–2021)

**Alexander Smith** (6 February 1930[[1]](#cite_note-1) – July 2021) was a [British](/wiki/British_people "British people") [trade unionist](/wiki/Trade_unionist "Trade unionist").

Smith studied with the Clothing Institute in 1958, before becoming prominent in the [National Union of Tailors and Garment Workers](/wiki/National_Union_of_Tailors_and_Garment_Workers "National Union of Tailors and Garment Workers") (NUTGW).[[2]](#cite_note-2) In 1974, he was elected Assistant Secretary of the union, then in 1979, he was elected as General Secretary.[[3]](#cite_note-3)[[4]](#cite_note-2021obit-4)

In 1991, Smith led the union into a merger with the [GMB Union](/wiki/GMB_Union "GMB Union").[[5]](#cite_note-5) He then became National Officer of the GMB, and served a term as [President of the Trades Union Congress](/wiki/President_of_the_Trades_Union_Congress "President of the Trades Union Congress").[[6]](#cite_note-6) He died in 2021.[[4]](#cite_note-2021obit-4)
